user_message: does this mean paid acquisition is viable, or is CAC the bottleneck?
assistant_message:
Yes, the economics say paid acquisition is viable to test; CAC is not the obvious bottleneck right now. The core acquisition read is CAC, gross profit, and payback period together [how-businesses-make-money:2]. Here, the snapshot has about $1,000 CAC and about $10,000 first-30-day gross profit, so first-month gross profit is far above acquisition cost. That matches the client-financed acquisition threshold: first-30-day gross profit greater than CAC [cfa:0].

The caveat is that the $1,000 CAC came from referral partners, not yet from paid ads. So I would interpret this as permission to run a controlled paid-acquisition test, not proof that every paid channel will work. The bottleneck to watch is whether paid traffic can hold CAC low enough and payback fast enough; shorter payback is what makes advertising easier to scale [payback-period:0].
